life cycle inventory for platinum. Derived from Glaister and Mudd (https://doi.org/10.1016/j.mineng.2009.12.007) which reports impacts in terms of energy adn water consumption per kg "platinum group metal" . Therefore, this same inventory is assumed for the other metals (iridium, rhodium,etc.).  Biosphere flows such as land use and mineral use is derived from platinum group metal association (PGMA) inventory data that was shared upon request. These emissions are specific to each metal. The emissions to water are based on the total quantity of sulfidic tailings output from the PGMA, and cross-referenced with the inventory from DOKA (https://doka.ch/SulfidicTailingsDisposalDoka.pdf) for sulfidic mine tailings.

life cycle inventory for platinum. Derived from Glaister and Mudd (https://doi.org/10.1016/j.mineng.2009.12.007) which reports impacts in terms of energy adn water consumption per kg "platinum group metal" . Therefore, this same inventory is assumed for the other metals (iridium, rhodium,etc.).  Biosphere flows such as land use and mineral use is derived from platinum group metal association (PGMA) inventory data that was shared upon request. These emissions /resources are specific to each metal. The emissions to water are based on the total quantity of sulfidic tailings output from the PGMA, and cross-referenced with the inventory from DOKA (https://doka.ch/SulfidicTailingsDisposalDoka.pdf) for sulfidic mine tailings.

process_description:
This inventory is for the production of refined Copper from sulfide ore (2% grade). Based on report by Argonne National lab on LCI of critical minerals/materials (Krishnamoorthy and Kelly, 2022)

- material left out is ammonium nitrate (used in quantity of 0.375 kg/kg), and Baryte (BaSO4) in 0.0015 kg/kg, graphite electrodes at 0.0002, copper powder (0.0002648), nitric acid (0.0001817), hydrogen peroxide (0.0024 kg/kg)

mining is 81% diesel, 19% electricity
benefication is 100% electricty 

biosphere flows are derived from : https://doi.org/10.1016/j.resconrec.2019.03.020

life cycle inventory for platinum. Derived from Glaister and Mudd (https://doi.org/10.1016/j.mineng.2009.12.007) which reports impacts in terms of energy adn water consumption per kg "platinum group metal" . Therefore, this same inventory is assumed for the other metals (iridium, rhodium,etc.).  Biosphere flows such as land use and mineral use is derived from platinum group metal association (PGMA) inventory data that was shared upon request. These emissions are specific to each metal. The emissions to water are based on the total quantity of sulfidic tailings output from the PGMA, and cross-referenced with the inventory from DOKA (https://doka.ch/SulfidicTailingsDisposalDoka.pdf) for sulfidic mine tailings.

life cycle inventory for platinum. Derived from Glaister and Mudd (https://doi.org/10.1016/j.mineng.2009.12.007) which reports impacts in terms of energy adn water consumption per kg "platinum group metal" . Therefore, this same inventory is assumed for the other metals (iridium, rhodium,etc.).  Biosphere flows such as land use and mineral use is derived from platinum group metal association (PGMA) inventory data that was shared upon request. These emissions are specific to each metal. The emissions to water are based on the total quantity of sulfidic tailings output from the PGMA, and cross-referenced with the inventory from DOKA (https://doka.ch/SulfidicTailingsDisposalDoka.pdf) for sulfidic mine tailings.

Therefore, this same inventory is assumed for the other metals (iridium, rhodium,etc.).  Biosphere flows such as land use and mineral use is derived from platinum group metal association (PGMA) inventory data that was shared upon request. These emissions are specific to each metal. The emissions to water are based on the total quantity of sulfidic tailings output from the PGMA, and cross-referenced with the inventory from DOKA (https://doka.ch/SulfidicTailingsDisposalDoka.pdf) for sulfidic mine tailings.

life cycle inventory for platinum. Derived from Glaister and Mudd (https://doi.org/10.1016/j.mineng.2009.12.007) which reports impacts in terms of energy adn water consumption per kg "platinum group metal" . Therefore, this same inventory is assumed for the other metals (iridium, rhodium,etc.).  Biosphere flows such as land use and mineral use is derived from platinum group metal association (PGMA) inventory data that was shared upon request. These emissions /resources are specific to each metal. The emissions to water are based on the total quantity of sulfidic tailings output from the PGMA, and cross-referenced with the inventory from DOKA (https://doka.ch/SulfidicTailingsDisposalDoka.pdf) for sulfidic mine tailings.

Biosphere flows such as land use and mineral use is derived from platinum group metal association (PGMA) inventory data that was shared upon request. These emissions /resources are specific to each metal. The emissions to water are based on the total quantity of sulfidic tailings output from the PGMA, and cross-referenced with the inventory from DOKA (https://doka.ch/SulfidicTailingsDisposalDoka.pdf) for sulfidic mine tailings.
